## Structure

TCP C→S 0x8480 — `Auth`. Client's login request carrying the
encryption key, an install/version fingerprint, and the
encrypted username + password. Verified 2026-05-10 against
samples from 17/17 retail captures. Body is 65 bytes (the
catalog samples truncate at 32B; full wire from pcap below).

```
[0..1]    84 80                  TCP opcode (constant)
[2]       enc_key u8             per-session encryption key (= 0x29
                                  in dominant retail; varies in
                                  other observations like 0x1c, 0x45)
[3..9]    install_hash 7B        CONSTANT `23 be 84 e1 6c d6 ae` —
                                  client EXE hash / install
                                  fingerprint
[10]      version_flag u8        varies: 0x28 OR 0xfe (likely
                                  client-version / build-flag)
[11..12]  version_pair u16       0x0103 OR 0x0102 LE (two values
                                  observed; 0x0103 dominant in
                                  newer captures, 0x0102 in older)
[13..20]  session_nonce 8B       per-session random (varies every
                                  attempt within capture set)
[21..22]  reserved u16           CONSTANT 0x0000
[23..24]  reserved u16           CONSTANT 0x00d0 (=208)
[25..32]  reserved 8B            CONSTANT `a5 ac 54 43 81 89 0d 0d`
[33..34]  username_len LE16      length of null-terminated username
                                  (in bytes, including null)
[35..36]  password_len LE16      length of encrypted password in
                                  bytes (= 2× char count)
[37..N]   username CString       null-terminated ASCII
[N+1..]   password encrypted     XOR-cipher with `enc_key`; client
                                  encodes 1 char → 2 bytes
```

Sample retail bytes (full 65B body):

```
84 80 29  23be84e16cd6ae 28 0301 b025638a738629f6 0000 d000 a5ac544381890d 0d
                       ^vf ^vp ^session_nonce        ^const  ^const         ^const
09 00  12 00  6d736e33776f6c6600  c29999f9b1b959e973a94b39b7054e9944 59 00
^uLen  ^pLen ^username "msn3wolf\0"  ^encrypted password (19B)
   CREATION_LEVELING_LONG — username "msn3wolf"

84 80 29  23be84e16cd6ae fe 0201 7d5c859806b7475c 0000 d000 a5ac544381890d 0d
09 00  12 00  6d736e33776f6c6600  c29999f9b1b959e973a94b39b7054e9944 59 00
   AUGUSTO — same username, different version_flag (0xfe) and version_pair (0201)
```

Note: same username "msn3wolf" + same encrypted password block
across 17/17 captures (this is a single test account used for
all retail recordings). The session_nonce at [13..20] does
vary per-capture / per-attempt.

## Variants

Single 65B form. Fields vary:
- `enc_key` at [2]: per-attempt random key.
- `version_flag` at [10] + `version_pair` at [11..12]: pair
  observed as `(0x28, 0x0103)` and `(0xfe, 0x0102)` — splits
  along older/newer client builds.
- `session_nonce` at [13..20]: per-attempt random.
- `username` and `password` lengths: vary with the actual
  credentials.

The fixed 7B install hash (`23be84e16cd6ae`) at [3..9] is
byte-identical across ALL 17 retail captures regardless of
account / character / time — making it a stable fingerprint of
the NC2 Evolution client EXE.

## Observed contexts

Emitted exactly once per gameserver login. Top marker `RESUME`
correlates with the resume-login flow.

50 observations / 17 captures. Each session sees this 2-6
times — the duplication suggests retransmission within a
single login burst (TCP layer retries) or multiple session
attempts.

The infoserver flavor (`server.infoserver.packets.client_tcp.Auth`)
shares the same 0x8480 opcode but is a different listener.

## Open questions

- The 30-byte reserved region at [3..32]: 8B is constant install
  hash, 8B is constant trailer, 2B is constant `00 00`, 2B is
  constant `d0 00` — but the version_flag (1B), version_pair
  (2B), and session_nonce (8B) DO vary. The fixed reserved bytes
  may include additional client-version / cipher-config data
  not yet decoded.
- The pair `(version_flag, version_pair) = (0x28, 0x0103)` vs
  `(0xfe, 0x0102)` — exact correspondence to NC2 patch level
  not yet pinned.
- Why does retail emit this packet 2-6 times per session in
  some captures? Likely client-side TCP retry / dual-server
  fan-out (gameserver + infoserver), but the higher-frequency
  captures (CHARDEL_SUBWAY ×6, VEHICLE_DRONE ×6) suggest a
  more interactive auth ping.

## Server-side handler

`server.gameserver.packets.client_tcp.Auth` — parses
encryption key, skips the 30-byte reserved region, decodes
username + encrypted password, and triggers the post-Auth
sequence:

1. `AuthAck` (0x8381) — auth acknowledgement.
2. `SessionReady` (0xa001) — session-ready signal.
3. `CharList` (0x8385) — character list.

The 30-byte reserved is treated as opaque (not validated) —
this is sufficient for client state advancement but does not
verify install integrity. Companion handler:
`server.infoserver.packets.client_tcp.Auth` — same parser,
different downstream emission.

Edge-case validation:
- `usernameLength` bounds-checked to (0, 256].
- `passwordLength / 2` bounds-checked to [0, 256].
- On any parsing/validation failure: emits
  `RequestFailed("ERROR")` instead of `AuthAck`.
